items.title: Consumer Advocates Question Whitmer's Voluntary Data Center Pledges
items.dek: Groups say the governor's approach falls short without binding rules to ensure tech companies cover full electricity and grid upgrade costs.
items.judge_note: New angle: consumer advocates criticizing the voluntary nature of Whitmer's pledges adds substantive pushback not yet covered.

items.title: Saline Township rejects 50% tax break for big data center
items.dek: Trustees turned down the abatement request tied to a major campus plan. Local taxpayers avoid subsidizing the project for years. The vote keeps full property-tax revenue flowing to schools and services.
items.source_title: Saline Township trustees vote against proposed 50% tax abatement for data center - MSN
items.judge_note: Clear new vote outcome—trustees rejected a tax abatement—distinct from the OpenAI/Oracle tax break stories already live.

items.title: Oracle data center in Saline Township now valued at $16 billion
items.dek: Developers locked in financing for the much larger campus. Washtenaw County could lose years of property tax revenue if the incentive package is approved. The project would also draw hundreds of megawatts from the regional grid.
items.source_title: Saline Township data center price tag balloons to $16B as developers lock in financing - AOL.com
items.judge_note: Concrete new development: financing locked in and new $16B valuation for Oracle-Saline project represents material progress beyond the earlier tax break stories

items.title: Saline Fights $16B Data Center Over Water and Farmland
items.dek: Township lost rezoning lawsuit, allowing Oracle-OpenAI project to break ground with $14M in benefits; residents say big tech steamrolls locals on utilities and land.
items.judge_note: New development: township lost rezoning lawsuit allowing project to proceed with $14M benefits package, a concrete legal/procedural outcome not previously repor
